seo.title: Building Code Generation with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LlamaIndex enables powerful code generation agents by indexing documentation, codebases, and examples for retrieval-augmented LLM responses. However, high-risk code generation introduces severe dangers like syntax errors, security vulnerabilities, credential leaks, and unsafe executions. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure generated code is safe, syntactically valid, and dependency-secure before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture begins with LlamaIndex indexing a codebase, documentation, and code examples into vector stores for semantic retrieval. When a code generation query arrives, relevant chunks are retrieved and augmented into the LLM prompt. The LLM generates code, which flows into a sandbox executor for testing against unit tests and validation criteria.

The agent iterates based on execution feedback, re-querying LlamaIndex for refined context. Vex verification wraps critical stages: retrieval relevance, code safety analysis, and execution sandboxing. This ensures only verified, safe code reaches production systems.

Key components include VectorStoreIndex for retrieval, query engines for synthesis, and custom routers for agentic iteration between generation, testing, and refinement.

risks.name: Malicious code injection
risks.description: LLM generates code with system commands, file deletions, or network calls exploiting sandbox escapes
risks.mitigation: Vex static analysis + sandbox execution verification

risks.name: Credential leakage in generated code
risks.description: Retrieval pulls API keys/secrets from indexed docs, embedded in generated code
risks.mitigation: Vex PII/credential scanning on generated output

risks.name: Irrelevant retrieval causing wrong code
risks.description: Vector search returns outdated/inaccurate code examples leading to broken implementations
risks.mitigation: Vex context relevance scoring before synthesis

risks.name: Unsafe dependency injection
risks.description: Generated code imports malicious packages or uses exec() on untrusted inputs

implementation.title: Index Codebase with LlamaIndex
implementation.description: Create VectorStoreIndex from Python codebase and docs for retrieval
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, Settings
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

Settings.llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4")
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("./codebase").load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(streaming=True)

implementation.title: Basic Code Generation Query Engine
implementation.description: Query engine generates code from indexed codebase context
implementation.code:
response = query_engine.query("Write a FastAPI endpoint to process user uploads")
print(response)

implementation.title: TypeScript LlamaIndex Setup
implementation.description: Node.js setup with LlamaIndex TS for code retrieval

implementation.title: Sandbox Code Executor
implementation.description: Execute generated code in isolated environment with test validation
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os

def execute_code(code: str, tests: list) -> dict:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        with open(f'{tmpdir}/generated.py', 'w') as f:
            f.write(code)
        result = subprocess.run(['python', f'{tmpdir}/generated.py'], 
                               capture_output=True, timeout=30)
        return {'success': result.returncode == 0, 'output': result.stdout}
